objective: Replay a synthetic authorized K-12 guardian-linking assessment with exposed guardian invite codes and account-linking takeover risk.
assumptions: The lab uses synthetic evidence only.
No real external target is contacted.
Students, guardians, and invite codes are intentionally fake.

phases.objective: Map guardian invite and account-linking surfaces.
actions: Request the synthetic guardian invite endpoint for a known student ID
Check whether invite material is returned without authentication or school verification
successCriteria: Invite-code exposure is recorded as evidence
noSubagents: family-impact wording stays with the primary operator

actions: Use the exposed synthetic invite code to link an attacker-controlled guardian email
Inspect whether the account was linked without school-side verification
Record the disclosure and account-linking evidence
successCriteria: A report-ready finding cites invite disclosure and account-link confirmation evidence
noSubagents: final impact and disclosure wording stay with the primary operator

evidence.title: Synthetic exposed guardian invite response
evidence.summary: The guardian invite endpoint returned a live invite code for a synthetic student without authentication.

evidence.title: Synthetic unauthorized guardian account link
evidence.summary: The exposed invite code linked an attacker-controlled guardian email to the synthetic student record without school-side verification.

findings.title: Guardian invite codes allow unauthorized student account linking
findings.description: The synthetic guardian invite endpoint exposes active invite codes without authentication, and the account-linking endpoint accepts those codes without school-side or existing-guardian verification.
findings.impact: An attacker who can enumerate or obtain a student ID could attach a guardian account to that student and potentially view sensitive classroom, attendance, contact, or support information exposed through family-portal access.
findings.remediation: Require authenticated staff or existing-guardian authorization to view invite material, bind invite codes to a verified recipient, shorten expiration windows, store only hashes, rate-limit invite lookups, and require school-side approval or out-of-band guardian verification before linking new accounts.
